response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer with strong web, mobile, and distributed-systems experience, but the role demands deep embedded firmware skills that are largely absent from the resume. Core hard requirements are unmet: there is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no Linux kernel investigation or source modification experience, and only limited C exposure (the C/C++ work at National Instruments was a desktop IDE, not embedded UNIX development). The security and networking specializations (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer-2/3 switching, NETCONF/SNMP, chip SDKs) are also missing. The mismatch on must-have qualifications outweighs the candidate's clear general engineering strength.
strengths: Broad, long-standing software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware projects
Strong testing and craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, pairing), valuable for quality-focused firmware teams
Some low-level C/C++ exposure and Linux/JACK audio systems work indicating capacity to learn systems-level topics
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement for this role
Lacks Linux kernel analysis/modification and embedded networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips)
Primary expertise is in JS/TS/DLang web stacks rather than C-based UNIX/embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: Missing Linux kernel & embedded security/networking experience
Strong general software engineering and testing background
